submissions.biggestGap: The biggest gap is domain knowledge and bridging the business logic with technical execution. Because I am new to the team, my reliance on templates and external help stems largely from not yet fully understanding the underlying business rules, edge cases, and user expectations. I cannot effectively orchestrate agents, if I don't deeply understand the workflow i'm supposed to automate
submissions.plannedActions: Deep dive into one core business workflow. Instead of trying to learn everything at once, I will sit down with a subject matter expert (or review existing documentation/code) to map out one specific, critical business process end-to-end. My goal is to successfully translate that human-level business logic into clear system constraints and prompts for an agent, proving I understand why the rules exist.

submissions.biggestGap:
I rated long-running agents as 3 because, if we consider a long-running process as something that takes 4+ hours, I haven’t really had a use case where I could apply this type of skill effectively.

That said, the pattern of unattended tasks with explicit success/failure criteria and self-verification is something I consistently use in short- and mid-running processes.
submissions.plannedActions: Over the next month, I want to run one small experiment applying this pattern to a real task: define the agent roles, handoffs, shared artifacts, and success/failure checks. I don’t need a perfect orchestration system yet, but I want to start practicing the pattern more intentionally.
